description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden barrel with a classic, slightly bulging cylindrical shape and a flat top opening. The exterior is composed of dark brown wooden staves, vertically aligned and bound together by two prominent black metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood grain is visible, giving the surface a natural, slightly matte appearance, while the metal bands provide structural reinforcement and a subtle contrast in texture and color. The interior appears hollow, suggesting it is designed to hold or store materials, and the open top indicates it is currently uncovered. There are no visible handles, markings, or decorative elements, making it a utilitarian object likely intended for storage or transport in urban or industrial settings.
description: A dark wooden barrel with black metal hoops and an open top, featuring visible wood grain and a utilitarian design.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from the front, showing the open top, wood grain, and both metal hoops clearly.
description_view_1: This view is similar to the front but slightly rotated, maintaining visibility of the open top and both hoops.
description_view_2: The barrel is viewed from another side, with the same features visible and no additional markings or handles.
description_view_3: A further rotated view, again showing the open top, wood texture, and metal hoops without any new features.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: black metal hoops at top and bottom
